evidence.text: In the U.S., Business Investment and Exports are two major indicators of MRO spending. Export growth has improved, as exports have responded to improved economic growth among countries that the U.S. exports to.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated pricing power, particularly in the U.S., by successfully increasing prices to offset costs. evidence.text: The business also increased prices to offset foreign exchange-related costs of goods sold inflation in the first quarter, but most customers are under contract and will not experience price increases until later in the year.

Grainger’s sales in the U.S. and Canada tend to positively correlate with Business Investment, Business Inventory, Exports and Industrial Production. In the U.S., sales tend to positively correlate with Gross Domestic Product (GDP). In Canada, sales tend to positively correlate with oil prices.
